title: TLS, Virtual Hosts, Credentials, and Authorization · Go Retrieval
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs tls, virtual hosts, credentials, and authorization from producer, broker, queue, delivery, Go runtime, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one protocol or concurrency misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a Go TLS and least-privilege connection contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use TLS with trusted CA, hostname and SNI verification, minimum protocol policy, client identity where required, and tested certificate rotation; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Choose generated credentials, x509 EXTERNAL, or OAuth 2 identity with explicit broker, client, token, renewal, and revocation support; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Grant minimum configure, write, and read regex permissions per virtual host and separately evaluate topic authorization; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Tenant Isolation Abuse
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go messaging team handles case 3759 during stream-versus-queue architecture board. Build deterministic Go evidence to verify TLS and vhost identity, redact secrets, and compute least-privilege permissions; bound payload, queue, confirm-ledger, and handler bytes; then make one state notification stale after recovery generation changes. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Defend tenant topology, message size, route, rate, queue count, headers, permissions, and noisy-neighbor budgets without trusting publisher metadata; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Inspect the Tenant Isolation Abuse trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Tenant Isolation Abuse.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for defend tenant topology, message size, route, rate, queue count, headers, permissions, and noisy-neighbor budgets without trusting publisher metadata; in go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence. For RabbitMQ and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Tenant Isolation Abuse, keep Tenant Isolation Abuse connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Tenant Isolation Abuse.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Tenant Isolation Abuse, keep Tenant Isolation Abuse connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend tenant topology, message size, route, rate, queue count, headers, permissions, and noisy-neighbor budgets without trusting publisher metadata; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

hints: For RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Tenant Isolation Abuse, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Tenant Isolation Abuse precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Tenant Isolation Abuse, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A review Go messaging team handles case 3477 during outbox relay recovery after a database commit. Build deterministic Go evidence to verify TLS and vhost identity, redact secrets, and compute least-privilege permissions; retain screen-reader-readable topology and delivery evidence; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Defend tenant topology, message size, route, rate, queue count, headers, permissions, and noisy-neighbor budgets without trusting publisher metadata; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Tenant Isolation Abuse, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A tenant header is sufficient authorization for routing and consuming tenant data. In Go, tls.Config with InsecureSkipVerify is acceptable inside a private network.
